seo.title: Building Agentic RAG with OpenAI Agents SDK + Vex Guardrails
seo.description: Learn how to build agentic rag with OpenAI Agents SDK + Vex guardrails. Detect failures, prevent drift, and ensure reliability.

content.intro: Building Agentic RAG with OpenAI's Agents SDK enables multi-agent systems that autonomously plan retrieval queries, fetch from multiple sources, and synthesize attributed responses. However, complex handoffs between planning, retrieval, and synthesis agents introduce risks like infinite loops, hallucinations, and unverified citations. Vex provides essential output guardrails to verify source faithfulness, detect hallucinations, and ensure cross-source consistency before responses reach users.

architectureOverview.heading: Agentic RAG Architecture with OpenAI Agents SDK
architectureOverview.description:
The Agentic RAG system uses OpenAI Agents SDK to orchestrate specialized agents: a Query Planner decomposes user questions into sub-queries, Retrieval Agents fetch from vector stores and web sources, a Synthesis Agent combines results with attribution, and a Validator ensures quality. Agents handoff via the SDK's structured messaging, with built-in tracing for debugging. This modular design scales to complex queries but amplifies failure modes without guardrails.

Each agent exposes tools (e.g., vector_search, web_search) and uses the SDK's handoff protocol for delegation. The Runner orchestrates execution, capturing intermediate states. Vex integrates as output parsers on each agent's final response before handoff or user delivery.
architectureOverview.diagram:
User Query
    ↓
[Query Planner Agent] → sub-queries
    ↓
[Retrieval Agent A] ─┬─→ Vector DB
[Retrieval Agent B] ─┤─→ Web Search
    ↓
[Synthesis Agent] ← citations + chunks
    ↓ + Vex Guardrails
Final Attributed Response

risks.name: Agent handoff loops between specialized agents
risks.description: Query Planner endlessly delegates to Retrieval Agents without convergence criteria
risks.mitigation: Vex query planning accuracy checks enforce termination conditions

risks.name: Guardrail bypasses via prompt injection
risks.description: Malicious user inputs trick synthesis agent into ignoring retrieval constraints
risks.mitigation: Vex input sanitization and faithfulness verification at each handoff

risks.name: Tool output not validated before agent consumption
risks.description: Retrieval agents pass unverified chunks directly to synthesis without format/content checks
risks.mitigation: Vex schema validation on tool outputs before agent processing

risks.name: Tracing gaps in multi-agent handoff chains
risks.description: Lost visibility into which agent failed during synthesis or citation generation
risks.mitigation: Vex end-to-end tracing with agent-specific checkpoints

implementation.title: Query Planning Agent Setup
implementation.description: Initialize the query decomposition agent with planning tools and Vex guardrails
implementation.code:
from agents import Agent, Runner
from vex import Vex

planner = Agent(
    name="Query Planner",
    instructions="Decompose complex questions into 2-4 precise retrieval sub-queries",
    tools=[query_decomposition_tool],
    output_parser=Vex([
        "query_planning_accuracy",
        "no_infinite_loops"
    ])
)

retrieval_team = [retrieval_agent_a, retrieval_agent_b]

runner = Runner([planner, *retrieval_team])

implementation.title: Multi-Source Retrieval Agent
implementation.description: Agent that coordinates vector and web retrieval with source tracking
implementation.code:
retrieval_agent = Agent(
    name="Multi-Source Retriever",
    instructions="Execute sub-queries across vector DB and web. Track source_id for each chunk",
    tools=[
        vector_search_tool,
        web_search_tool,
        format_chunks_tool
    ],
    handoff_to=[synthesis_agent],
    output_parser=Vex([
        "source_faithfulness",
        "chunk_schema_validation"
    ])
)

implementation.title: Synthesis Agent with Attribution
implementation.description: Combines retrieved chunks with mandatory source citations
implementation.code:
synthesis_agent = Agent(
    name="Response Synthesizer",
    instructions="Synthesize comprehensive answer using ONLY provided chunks. Cite sources with [source_id] markers",
    tools=[citation_formatter],
    output_parser=Vex([
        "hallucination_detection",
        "citation_verification",
        "cross_source_consistency"
    ])
)

implementation.title: End-to-End Agentic RAG Runner
implementation.description: Orchestrate full pipeline with tracing and error recovery
implementation.code:
async def run_agentic_rag(query: str):
    with Vex.trace("agentic_rag_pipeline"):
        result = await runner.run(query)
        
        # Vex final validation
        final_check = Vex([
            "source_faithfulness",
            "hallucination_detection",
            "query_planning_accuracy"
        ]).validate(result.final_output)
        
        if final_check.passed:
            return result.final_output
        else:
            return "Response failed quality checks: " + final_check.errors

vexIntegration.title: Source Faithfulness Guardrail
vexIntegration.description: Verify synthesis only uses provided retrieval chunks
vexIntegration.code:
from vex import Guardrail

source_faithfulness = Guardrail(
    name="source_faithfulness",
    spec={
        "type": "semantic_similarity",
        "retrieval_chunks": "{{chunks}}",
        "synthesis_text": "{{response}}",
        "threshold": 0.85,
        "fail_action": "reject"
    }
)

# Integrated in agent output_parser
planner.output_parser = Vex([source_faithfulness])

vexIntegration.title: Hallucination Detection on Synthesis
vexIntegration.description: Detect content not grounded in retrieval sources
vexIntegration.code:
hallucination_detector = Guardrail(
    name="hallucination_detection",
    spec={
        "type": "grounded_generation",
        "input": "{{user_query}}",
        "sources": "{{retrieval_chunks}}",
        "output": "{{synthesis}}",
        "max_hallucination_ratio": 0.05
    }
)

synthesis_agent.output_parser = Vex([
    hallucination_detector,
    source_faithfulness
])

vexIntegration.title: Citation Verification Guardrail
vexIntegration.description: Ensure all claims in response have corresponding source citations
vexIntegration.code:
citation_verifier = Guardrail(
    name="citation_verification",
    spec={
        "type": "citation_check",
        "response": "{{final_output}}",
        "citations": "{{source_mapping}}",
        "require_all_claims_cited": True,
        "valid_citation_formats": ["[1]", "[source_id]"]
    }
)

# Final gate before user delivery
final_vex = Vex([
    citation_verifier,
    "cross_source_consistency"
])

cta.heading: Secure Your OpenAI Agents SDK Agentic RAG
cta.description: Add Vex guardrails today to eliminate hallucination risks and ensure production-grade Agentic RAG reliability.
